codeReview.summary: The feature establishes the requested board structures, FEN parser/serializer, and Zobrist tables, but it misses the feature's exact round-trip guarantee: `position_from_fen()` accepts non-canonical castling-rights orderings and `position_to_fen()` always rewrites them in canonical `KQkq` order. Because accepted inputs can change when reserialized, the implementation does not fully satisfy the promised FEN round-trip behavior.

issues.description: `position_from_fen()` accepts any unique castling-right letters in any order (`qk`, `kQ`, etc.), but `position_to_fen()` always serializes rights back out in fixed `KQkq` order (`engine/src/position.c:458-479`). An input such as `4k3/8/8/8/8/8/8/4K3 w qk - 0 1` therefore parses successfully and reserializes as `... w kq - 0 1`, which violates the feature's required exact FEN round-trip behavior for accepted inputs.

`test_all_castling_right_combinations()` only generates canonical castling strings via `append_castling_string()`, so the suite never exercises the parser/serializer mismatch on other castling-right spellings that `position_from_fen()` currently accepts. That coverage gap is why the round-trip bug above slipped through.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared `engine-worker` skill no longer matches the mission's actual engine layout. It still instructs workers to read/write `tests/` and `engine/` root files and compile with `engine/*.c tests/*.c`, while this mission documents and uses `engine/src/` and `engine/tests/`.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/engine-worker/SKILL.md:35-64 vs /Users/aravhawk/.factory/missions/70ca98e6-cb3b-4e1c-9eb9-4d887ed17139/AGENTS.md:38-46; the reviewed transcript skeleton for worker session 0eaff8ec-2abd-4255-b9f0-b9353816246f shows the worker creating `engine/src` and `engine/tests` and compiling `engine/src/*.c engine/tests/*.c`.

summary: Reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `c502e28` for `engine-board-representation`. Result: fail, because the FEN parser accepts castling-right spellings that the serializer rewrites, breaking the feature's exact round-trip guarantee; I also noted that the shared `engine-worker` skill is out of sync with the mission's `engine/src` / `engine/tests` layout.
